title: Salsa on Cotton
stainChemistry: Salsa is treated as a mixed food/sauce stain: tomato/tannin or dye-like color plus possible oil, spice, and sugar. The supported Home path is detergent-first and conservative; this card does not claim precise salsa/chile chemistry or that every salsa stain requires a dry-side-first sequence.
whyThisWorks: Lifting solids and blotting liquid reduce load without rubbing. Cool back-rinsing moves water-soluble sauce components away from the face of the fabric, while generic liquid/heavy-duty detergent provides surfactant action for mixed food and any oily residue. Air-drying before heat prevents remaining red/orange color or sugar/oil residue from setting.
scienceNote: Heat can worsen some food/sugar residues, so avoid dryer/iron/heat until inspection shows the stain is gone. No salsa-specific chile/oil mechanism is claimed.
defaultAssumption: Use for washable cotton. Colored cotton needs a hidden seam dye-transfer test and lower-temperature caution; dry-clean-only, valuable, or unstable-dye items should escalate.

spottingProtocol.technique: Care-label, construction, fiber, and colorfastness assessment
spottingProtocol.equipment: Clean white cloth, hidden seam/hem test
spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Stop immediately on transfer or change
spottingProtocol.instruction: Confirm cotton is safe for wet treatment and document any Home attempts. Test colorfastness on a hidden seam or hem before any spotting.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: Pro-only intake. Follow care label; reject or escalate unstable dye, structured construction, or high-value textiles.

spottingProtocol.technique: Use the mildest source-supported professional path first
spottingProtocol.equipment: Product-labeled detergent or family-specific spotter; clean towels
spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Use product-label dwell only; stop on dye movement or texture change
spottingProtocol.instruction: Treat salsa on cotton as a mixed food/sauce stain and begin with controlled detergent/water action before any stronger chemistry. Keep chemistry bounded by SDS/label and fabric test results.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: Pro-only. Use PPE/ventilation required by product SDS; do not expose Home users to pro solvents, acids, oxidizers, or concentrated spotters.

spottingProtocol.technique: Flush between products and inspect dry
spottingProtocol.equipment: Water/steam only if fabric and product label permit; drying rack
spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Until residues are flushed and fabric is dry enough to inspect
spottingProtocol.instruction: Flush or rinse out chemistry before switching products. Air-dry/inspect before considering any heat, oxidizer, solvent, or additional professional escalation.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: Pro-only. Do not mix incompatible chemistry; use PPE/ventilation and document any last-resort escalation.

products: Product-labeled professional sauce/tannin/oil systems only, used per SDS/label
professionalProtocol.temperature: Cool/cold first; warmer professional laundering only after care-label and colorfastness checks
warnings: Pro-only sauce/tannin/oil sequence requires SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, rinse/flush controls, and fabric testing. Do not expose Home users to acid spotters, solvents, oxidizers, or pro products.

homeSolutions.instruction: Check that the cotton is washable. Gently lift salsa solids with a spoon edge or dull knife. Do not rub the sauce into the weave.
Blot remaining liquid with a clean white cloth. Avoid spreading the red/orange area or any oily halo.
Rinse from the back with cool water if the care label allows. Do not hot-flush or use heat while mixed food residue remains.

homeSolutions.agent: Hidden Seam Test on Colored Cotton
homeSolutions.instruction: Test detergent and cool water on a hidden seam with a clean white cloth. Stop if dye transfers or the fabric changes.
Apply a small amount of generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent. Work gently; do not scrub. Launder according to the care label.

Air-dry before dryer or iron. Repeat the conservative cool rinse/detergent path once if improving. Escalate for persistent red/orange color, spreading oily halo, dye bleed/color loss, or no improvement after two rounds.

steps: 1. Care Label and Solids First: Check that the cotton is washable. Gently lift salsa solids with a spoon edge or dull knife. Do not rub the sauce into the weave.
2. Blot Liquid: Blot remaining liquid with a clean white cloth. Avoid spreading the red/orange area or any oily halo.
3. Cool Back-Rinse if Washable: Rinse from the back with cool water if the care label allows. Do not hot-flush or use heat while mixed food residue remains.
4. Hidden Seam Test on Colored Cotton: Test detergent and cool water on a hidden seam with a clean white cloth. Stop if dye transfers or the fabric changes.

5. Generic Liquid or Heavy-Duty Detergent: Apply a small amount of generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent. Work gently; do not scrub. Launder according to the care label.

6. Air-Dry and Inspect: Air-dry before dryer or iron. Repeat the conservative cool rinse/detergent path once if improving. Escalate for persistent red/orange color, spreading oily halo, dye bleed/color loss, or no improvement after two rounds.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Escalate persistent red/orange color, oily halo spreading, dye bleed/color loss, dry-clean-only/valuable/unstable-dye items, or no improvement after two conservative rounds.
warnings: No hot-flush or precise salsa/chile chemistry claims
No branded detergent or mandatory oil-first step
No routine Home acid spotters, peroxide, hot wash, tannin spotters, concentrated acids, solvents, or pro chemistry

neverDo: Never put the item in a dryer, use an iron, or apply heat until the stain is gone after air-drying inspection
Never rub or scrub aggressively; blot, lift, or gently work only enough to avoid driving soil deeper
Never mix cleaning agents, bleach, ammonia, acids, solvents, or professional spotters
Never use chlorine bleach unless the care label allows it and the fabric is white or confirmed colorfast
Never use professional-strength oxidizers, acids, dry-side solvents, rust/mineral removers, adhesive removers, or named pro products in the Home path
Never hot-flush salsa or use dryer/iron/heat while mixed food residue remains
Never claim all salsa needs an oil-first or dry-side solvent-first path
Never use routine Home acid spotters, peroxide, tannin spotters, concentrated acids, solvents, hot wash, or professional chemistry
fiberSensitivities: Cotton is cellulose; white cotton may tolerate more laundering than colored cotton
Colored cotton requires hidden seam colorfastness testing, lower-temperature caution, and no chlorine/strong peroxide default
chemicalBoundaries: Home path is limited to water, generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent, and care-label laundering
Use any detergent, enzyme detergent, or pretreat only according to its product label and only after a hidden-area test on colored fabric
Oxidizers are not routine Home steps; any trace-color escalation must be care-label-safe, white/colorfast, hidden-test-passed, and never mixed with other cleaners
Professional chemistry is Pro-only and requires SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, colorfastness testing, and flush/rinse controls
Home salsa path is cool water plus generic liquid/heavy-duty detergent only
If residual color escalation is ever considered, it must be care-label-safe, white/colorfast, hidden-test-passed, never mixed, and preferably handled by Pro
chemicalControls: Home path is limited to water, generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent, and care-label laundering
Use any detergent, enzyme detergent, or pretreat only according to its product label and only after a hidden-area test on colored fabric
Oxidizers are not routine Home steps; any trace-color escalation must be care-label-safe, white/colorfast, hidden-test-passed, and never mixed with other cleaners
Professional chemistry is Pro-only and requires SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, colorfastness testing, and flush/rinse controls
Home salsa path is cool water plus generic liquid/heavy-duty detergent only
If residual color escalation is ever considered, it must be care-label-safe, white/colorfast, hidden-test-passed, never mixed, and preferably handled by Pro
homeAllowed: Cool/cold water or care-label-safe lukewarm water
Generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent
proOnly: Professional spotters, dry-side solvents, acids, oxidizers, reducers, concentrated chemistry, steam, and heat escalation require SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, flush/rinse controls, and documented fabric testing
testFirst: Test detergent, warmer water, enzyme detergent, oxidizer escalation, alcohol/solvent, acid spotter, or any pro chemistry on a hidden seam/hem with a clean white cloth before treating the visible area
safetyMatrix.colorfastnessTest: On a hidden seam or hem, press a clean white cloth with the planned detergent/water mix for one minute; stop if dye transfers, color changes, or texture changes.
safetyMatrix.flushNeutralizeDry: Rinse or flush out each cleaning agent before changing products; air-dry fully and inspect before any dryer, iron, heat, or professional escalation.
safetyMatrix.temperatureLimits: Cool/cold first for mixed sauce/unknown components. Use care-label-safe lukewarm only if needed after initial removal; no dryer/iron/heat until gone.
escalationTriggers: Stop and escalate on dye bleed, color loss, texture change, ring formation, spreading halo, or fabric distortion
Stop and escalate dry-clean-only, non-washable, lined, structured, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye items
Escalate persistent red/orange color, spreading oily halo, dye bleed/color loss, dry-clean-only/valuable/unstable-dye items, or no improvement after two conservative rounds
stopAndEscalate: Stop and escalate on dye bleed, color loss, texture change, ring formation, spreading halo, or fabric distortion
Stop and escalate dry-clean-only, non-washable, lined, structured, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye items
Escalate persistent red/orange color, spreading oily halo, dye bleed/color loss, dry-clean-only/valuable/unstable-dye items, or no improvement after two conservative rounds
safetyMatrix.ppeVentilation: For Home detergent use, follow the product label and keep products away from children and pets. For concentrates or Pro chemistry, use gloves/eye protection as the SDS requires and work with ventilation.
safetyMatrix.homeVsProBoundary: Home guidance excludes professional-strength oxidizers, acids, solvents, named pro products, exact pro concentrations, steam, and heat escalation; those belong only in a professional workflow with SDS/label controls.

materialWarnings: Treat salsa as a mixed sauce stain, not one precise chemistry
Colored cotton requires hidden seam dye-transfer testing
Avoid heat until red/orange color and any oily halo are gone

escalation.when: Escalate persistent red/orange color, oily halo spreading, dye bleed/color loss, dry-clean-only/valuable/unstable-dye items, or no improvement after two conservative rounds.
escalation.whatToTell: Salsa on cotton; whether solids were lifted; whether cool back-rinse and detergent were used; any persistent red/orange color, oily halo, dye transfer, heat exposure, or prior products.
escalation.specialistType: professional textile cleaner or dry cleaner

contextQuestions: What kind of salsa/sauce and how old is it?
Is the cotton white or colored?
Was heat, peroxide, vinegar, dish soap, or a dryer used?
Is there a red/orange mark, oily halo, or both?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Keep as mixed-stain detergent-first guidance; avoid precise chili chemistry or mandatory oil-first instructions.

sources.title: University Stain Science (compiled extension reference)
sources.evidence: Consumer stain-family guidance for protein, tannin, oil, combination and unknown stains; blot rather than rub, work from the wrong side where appropriate, test first, and do not iron or dry until the stain is removed.

sources.title: EPA Alternative Cleaning Curriculum
sources.evidence: Safety and process gates: heat, concentration, oxidation, abrasion and mismanaged water can damage textiles; detergents/surfactants suspend oily soils; test hidden areas, follow product labels, ventilate, use PPE for concentrates, avoid incompatible mixes, and clean only according to care label or reject/escalate.

sources.title: GONR Material Safety Matrix v2
sources.evidence: Internal cotton/linen safety boundaries; protein, oil, tannin and oxidizer controls; dry-side solvent/pro chemistry boundaries; no hot water on protein stains; never mix chlorine bleach with ammonia or acids.

sources.title: R.R. Street 4Streets On-the-Spot
sources.evidence: Professional classification and operator workflow context for food/beverage, oil, tannin, protein and combination stains; pro-only product taxonomy.

sources.title: American Drycleaner Spotting Techniques
sources.evidence: Professional solvent/oil, tannin, flushing and controlled last-resort bleaching cautions; low suitability for Home copy.

sources.title: BÜFA DetaProfi Spotting Chart
sources.evidence: Professional chart support for oil, red wine, sauce/tannin and linen hem testing; rinse between products; pro-only chemistry sequence reference.

sources.title: BÜFA Professional Spotting Guide
sources.evidence: Professional training support for heat-sensitive food/sugar residues, protein coagulation/enzymes, and wine/fruit tannin classification; low suitability for Home copy.
